stem: A graph models the height above the ground y, in meters, of a small model rocket x seconds after it is launched from the top of a hill. The graph is a downward-opening parabola whose vertex is at the approximate point (1.4, 25) and whose x-intercept is at x = 3.2. What is the best interpretation of the x-intercept of the graph?

answerOptions.content: The rocket reaches a maximum height above the ground at 3.2 seconds.
The rocket reaches a maximum height above the ground at 1.4 seconds.
The rocket hits the ground at 1.4 seconds.
The rocket hits the ground at 3.2 seconds.

rationale: Choice D is correct. The x-intercept of the graph is the point where y = 0, i.e., where the rocket has zero height above the ground. That is the moment the rocket hits the ground, which occurs at x = 3.2 seconds. Choices A and B describe the maximum height (the vertex), not the x-intercept. Choice C uses the wrong time value.
